articles.title: San Francisco’s housing market has lost its mind
articles.summary: The invisible force behind all of this is no mystery to anyone paying attention to the city's tech economy. San Francisco is home to some of the most valuable private companies in the world, and their employees have been quietly accumulating — and, increasingly, cashing out — fortunes.

articles.title: Coinbase rebounds as altcoins surge with bitcoin holding above $80,000
Prime Video follows Netflix and Disney by adding a TikTok-like ‘Clips’ feed in its app
articles.summary: The Clips feed aims to enable discovery by offering users a scrollable feed with short snippets of shows and movies.

articles.title: AI agents fueled a frenzy of startup building at the Consensus Miami EasyA hackathon
Intel’s comeback story is even wilder than it seems
articles.summary: Intel's stock has risen a stunning 490% over the past year, a bet by Wall Street that may be running well ahead of the company's actual turnaround.

articles.title: SEC chair Atkins signals new rules for onchain markets, AI-driven finance
Cloudflare says AI made 1,100 jobs obsolete, even as revenue hit a record high
articles.summary: Cloudflare announced its first large-scale layoff. CEO Matthew Prince says because of AI efficiency gains, the company doesn't need as many support roles.

articles.title: Kraken parent goes for the OCC charter in bid to become a federal crypto bank
Porsche shutters e-bike, battery, software subsidiaries as part of company overhaul
articles.summary: More than 500 people will be affected by the closures. “We must refocus on our core business,” Porsche CEO and executive chair Michael Leiters said in a statement. "This is the indispensable foundation for a successful strategic realignment. This forces us to make painful cuts — including our subsidiaries."

articles.title: ECB's Lagarde’s digital euro warning: Why Europe shouldn’t just copy the U.S. stablecoin model
Expedia: Strong Execution, Expanding Margins, And Aggressive Buybacks Support Our Strong Buy Upgrade (Rating Upgrade)
Uber partner Avride is under investigation for self-driving crashes
articles.summary: The National Highway Traffic Safety Administration has opened an investigation into Avride after identifying more than a dozen crashes and one minor injury.
